description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, speckled stone material in between. The block has a solid, robust appearance, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the functional, utilitarian design. The overall look suggests durability and resistance to wear, suitable for high-traffic urban environments. The surface texture appears slightly rough, typical of cut or cast stone used in public infrastructure.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on top, creating a striped pattern and a sturdy, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the block's thickness, with grooves running vertically.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped pattern and the block's rectangular shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the groove depth and the block's solid, rectangular profile.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering visible; surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or moss.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
